title: The Big Show
story_html:
Ben had a big dream: to perform on the stage of the school theater. He got an important role — the class leader in the show — but the moment he found out, he began to worry.

Ben used to be late for practice because the walk from his apartment building took him past a busy block of shops every day. When he arrived, his friend Amy was already waiting.

Amy was calm and gentle. "It's okay to feel stupid about a new thing at first," she said. "Let's practice your lines together." Ben felt tough about the whole idea, but he picked up a pencil and started to write his lines down anyway.

At the first practice, Ben stood in the wrong spot and felt disappointed in himself. Amy did not laugh. She gently suggested something instead: "Try standing a little higher, near the light, and explain your first line slowly." She would gently correct him each time, and he kept a clear picture of the stage in his mind.

Every day after school, Ben decided to try again, and being responsible for his own practice slowly began to pay off.

On the day of the show, Ben felt a small attack of nerves backstage. But Amy was there, smiling. "You'll do great," she said.

Finally, it was Ben's turn. He walked to his position, took a deep breath, and gave his speech in a clear, proud voice. There was no fear now — only peace.

When he finished, everyone clapped, and his teacher called it one of the best of the year. It was a wonderful surprise. Ben was no longer the hardworking but nervous boy from the first practice.

That evening, his family drove him home, smiling the whole way.

proud.def: Feeling pleased about something you or someone else has done well.

disappointed.def: Feeling sad because something did not happen the way you hoped.

gentle.def: Kind, soft, and careful, not rough.

hardworking.def: Working with a lot of effort and energy.

responsible.def: Able to be trusted to do the right thing.

worry.def: To feel nervous about something that might go wrong.

pencil.def: A tool used for writing or drawing.

apartment.def: A set of rooms for living in, usually in a bigger building.

block.def: An area of a city surrounded by streets.

perform.def: To do something in front of an audience.

finally.def: After a long time; at last.

dream.def: Something you hope to achieve in the future.

decide.def: To choose something after thinking about it.

suggest.def: To offer an idea or a plan.

attack.def: A sudden action to hurt or damage something.

position.def: The place where something or someone is.

role.def: The part someone plays in a situation.

explain.def: To tell someone about something so they understand it.

peace.def: A state of calm with no fighting or arguing.

picture.def: A drawing or photo of something.

drive.def: To control and ride in a car.

speech.def: A talk given out loud to a group of people.

theater.def: A building where plays or shows are performed.

questions.qtext: In "Amy would gently correct him each time," what does correct mean?
options: to point out a mistake and help fix it
questions.explain: B — Amy helps Ben fix his mistakes kindly, not by criticizing him.

questions.qtext: Which word best replaces disappointed in "Ben stood in the wrong spot and felt disappointed in himself"?
questions.explain: B — disappointed means feeling sad because something didn't go the way you hoped.

questions.qtext: Amy says, "Let's practice your lines together." What is she suggesting?
options: an idea for how Ben can improve
questions.explain: B — suggest means to offer an idea or a plan.

questions.qtext: Why did Ben feel nervous before the show?
options: He worried about forgetting his lines
He was too tired to perform
questions.explain: B — Ben worried about forgetting his lines; practicing with Amy and her encouragement helped him feel calmer.

questions.qtext: How did Ben change from the start of the story to the end?
options: He went from confident to nervous
He stayed exactly the same the whole time
He went from worried and unsure to calm and proud
He decided never to perform again
questions.explain: C — He started out worried and felt stupid at his first practice, but ended up giving his speech in a clear, proud voice, feeling peace instead of fear.
